what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG information icon with a currentColor filled circle and a white vertical info mark inside it.
observations: Root svg uses viewBox="0 0 24 24" with no explicit width or height.
Default stroke is currentColor at 1.5 width with round caps and joins.
The circular badge is centered at 12,12 with radius 9, filled with currentColor and no stroke.
The inner mark is a single path containing a vertical line and dot segment stroked in #FFFFFF.
standouts: Combines theme-inherited outer color with a fixed white inner glyph.
The info mark is drawn as stroked path segments rather than text.
conventions: Uses common icon SVG defaults: fill="none", stroke="currentColor", round stroke caps, and round joins.
Overrides inherited stroke behavior for the inner glyph with a hard-coded white stroke.
concerns: The fixed #FFFFFF inner stroke assumes the currentColor circle provides adequate contrast.
No title, role, or aria attributes are present in the SVG itself.
notes: A compact filled info badge icon whose outer color inherits from context and whose inner mark stays white.
questions: Should the inner mark always remain white, or should it be theme-controlled?
Is accessible labeling supplied by the embedding context?
purpose_vs_actual: The file name suggests an info icon, and the SVG draws a circular information glyph.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a reusable information badge icon that visually follows surrounding color while preserving a white symbol inside.
novelty: The outer badge inherits currentColor while the inner info mark is fixed to white within the same SVG.
contribution: Contributes a concise filled information icon pattern with context-driven badge color and a stable inner glyph color for use as a design-system asset.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 inline SVG info icon made from a currentColor filled circle and a white stroked information mark.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing informational UI affordances as a reusable themed icon asset.
